name: Kaizen DMAIC Ontology (K-DMAIC-ONT)
description: Ontology for modelling Kaizen DMAIC — the compressed 3-5 day blitz variant of Six Sigma DMAIC. v2.0.0 deprecates kdmaic:WasteCategory (replaced by vana:WasteCategory) and kdmaic:ValueStreamMap (replaced by vsm:ValueStreamMap) as part of the PE-L6S sub-series extraction. All other entities unchanged. Backward-compatible aliases resolve deprecated entities to their imported equivalents.
oaa:domain: Lean Six Sigma, Kaizen Events, Rapid Process Improvement, Value Stream Mapping, Waste Elimination

creator.name: Platform Foundation Core - Ontology Architect Agent

oaa:imports.note: v2.0.0: Replaces deprecated kdmaic:ValueStreamMap with vsm:ValueStreamMap
v2.0.0: Replaces deprecated kdmaic:WasteCategory with vana:WasteCategory

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Gemba-to-QuickWin Lineage
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Trace waste from observation through to elimination — full lineage from Gemba walk to implemented quick win
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Answer: Which quick wins address the wastes observed during the Gemba walk?

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Experiment-to-Control Chain
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Trace a rapid experiment through its quick win to the visual control sustaining the improvement
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Answer: What visual controls sustain the improvements discovered through rapid experiments?

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Cross-Ontology Process Bridge
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Connect the Kaizen event to the PE-ONT process it improves and the metrics that track improvement
oaa:joinPatterns.joinPath: kdmaic:KaizenEvent → appliesToProcess → pe:Process → pe:measures → pe:ProcessMetric ← kdmaic:improvesMeasure ← kdmaic:KaizenEvent
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Answer: Which PE process metrics improved as a result of this Kaizen event?

entities.name: KaizenEvent
entities.description: A time-boxed rapid improvement event (Kaizen blitz) that compresses DMAIC into 3-5 days. A co-located team performs Gemba observation, value stream mapping, waste identification, rapid experiments, and same-day implementation of quick wins. Extends pe:Process semantics but adds Kaizen-specific attributes for event logistics, sigma tracking, and waste targeting.

oaa:properties.name: eventType
oaa:properties.description: Type of Kaizen event. Blitz: full 3-5 day co-located event. Mini: 1-2 day focused event. Virtual: remote Kaizen event.

oaa:properties.name: duration
oaa:properties.description: Duration of the event (P1D to P5D). Must conform to BR-K-001.

oaa:properties.name: teamSize
oaa:properties.description: Number of team members (advisory: 5-10 per BR-K-006)

oaa:properties.name: gembaLocation
oaa:properties.description: Physical or logical location where the process under improvement operates (the Gemba)

oaa:properties.name: sponsor
oaa:properties.description: Executive sponsor authorising the event

oaa:properties.name: facilitator
oaa:properties.description: Lean/Six Sigma facilitator leading the event

oaa:properties.name: targetProcess
oaa:properties.description: Name or ID of the process targeted for improvement

oaa:properties.name: priorSigma
oaa:properties.description: Sigma level before the Kaizen event

oaa:properties.name: postSigma
oaa:properties.description: Sigma level after the Kaizen event

entities.name: GembaObservation
entities.description: A structured observation recorded at the point of work (Gemba) during a Gemba walk. Observers go to where the work happens, watch the process in action, and record waste, delays, quality issues, and improvement opportunities. Each observation classifies exactly one waste type.

oaa:properties.name: location
oaa:properties.description: Specific location within the Gemba where observed

oaa:properties.name: observer
oaa:properties.description: Person who recorded the observation

oaa:properties.name: timestamp
oaa:properties.description: When the observation was recorded

oaa:properties.name: evidenceRef
oaa:properties.description: URI or path to supporting evidence (photo, video, etc.)

entities.name: WasteCategory
entities.oaa:deprecationNote: v2.0.0: Use vana:WasteCategory from VANA-ONT v1.0.0. Alias kdmaic:WasteCategory resolves to vana:WasteCategory for backward compatibility.
entities.description: One of the 8 Lean wastes using the DOWNTIME mnemonic: Defects, Overproduction, Waiting, Non-utilized talent, Transport, Inventory, Motion, Extra processing. Predefined instances are provided as testInstances. Custom waste categories can extend this set.

oaa:properties.name: wasteCode
oaa:properties.description: DOWNTIME mnemonic letter

oaa:properties.name: mnemonic
oaa:properties.description: Position in DOWNTIME mnemonic (e.g. 'D = Defects')

oaa:properties.name: examples
oaa:properties.description: Typical examples of this waste type

oaa:properties.name: detectionMethod
oaa:properties.description: How to detect this waste during a Gemba walk

entities.name: ValueStreamMap
entities.oaa:deprecationNote: v2.0.0: Use vsm:ValueStreamMap from VSM-ONT v1.0.0. Alias kdmaic:ValueStreamMap resolves to vsm:ValueStreamMap for backward compatibility.
entities.description: A current-state or future-state value stream map (VSM) that visualises the flow of materials and information through a process. Captures lead time, process time, value-added ratio, takt time, and bottleneck identification. Both current-state and future-state maps are required before the Improve phase (BR-K-005).

oaa:properties.name: processStepCount
oaa:properties.description: Number of process steps mapped

oaa:properties.name: totalLeadTime
oaa:properties.description: Total lead time from customer order to delivery

oaa:properties.name: totalProcessTime
oaa:properties.description: Sum of actual value-adding process time

oaa:properties.name: valueAddedRatio
oaa:properties.description: Ratio of process time to lead time (0.0-1.0). Higher is better.

oaa:properties.name: taktTime
oaa:properties.description: Available production time divided by customer demand rate

oaa:properties.name: bottleneck
oaa:properties.description: Identified bottleneck step or constraint

entities.name: RapidExperiment
entities.description: A quick try-storm test of a countermeasure during the Kaizen event. Unlike standard DMAIC DOE (days/weeks), rapid experiments are completed in hours. Each tests a specific hypothesis with a countermeasure, measures before/after, and decides whether to implement immediately as a quick win.

oaa:properties.name: hypothesis
oaa:properties.description: What we believe will happen if we apply the countermeasure

oaa:properties.name: countermeasure
oaa:properties.description: The change being tested to address the waste

oaa:properties.name: duration
oaa:properties.description: How long the experiment took (advisory: should not exceed P1D per BR-K-008)

oaa:properties.name: beforeMetric
oaa:properties.description: Measured value before countermeasure

oaa:properties.name: afterMetric
oaa:properties.description: Measured value after countermeasure

oaa:properties.name: implementImmediately
oaa:properties.description: Whether this experiment's countermeasure should be implemented same-day

entities.name: QuickWin
entities.description: An improvement implemented same-day during the Kaizen event. Quick wins are the tangible output of successful rapid experiments — they eliminate a specific waste and must be sustained by a visual control (BR-K-003).

oaa:properties.name: beforeState
oaa:properties.description: State before the quick win was implemented

oaa:properties.name: afterState
oaa:properties.description: State after the quick win was implemented

oaa:properties.name: effortLevel
oaa:properties.description: Implementation effort. High-effort changes are not quick wins — they become follow-up actions.

oaa:properties.name: implementedBy
oaa:properties.description: Person or team who implemented the quick win

oaa:properties.name: implementedDate
oaa:properties.description: Date the quick win was implemented (must be during event)

entities.name: VisualControl
entities.description: A visual management mechanism established during the Control phase to sustain quick wins. Visual controls make the standard visible so that deviations are immediately apparent. Every quick win must have at least one visual control (BR-K-003).

oaa:properties.name: location
oaa:properties.description: Where the visual control is installed

oaa:properties.name: owner
oaa:properties.description: Person responsible for maintaining the visual control

oaa:properties.name: checkFrequency
oaa:properties.description: How often the visual control is reviewed (e.g. 'daily', 'per-shift', 'weekly')

entities.name: FollowUpAction
entities.description: A post-event action item tracked during the 30-day follow-up period. Follow-up actions address items that could not be completed during the event or require longer-term monitoring. Deadlines must be within 30 days of event end (BR-K-004). Overdue actions must trigger escalation (BR-K-007).

oaa:properties.name: owner
oaa:properties.description: Person responsible for completing the action

oaa:properties.name: deadline
oaa:properties.description: Must be within 30 days of event end date (BR-K-004)

oaa:properties.name: verificationMethod
oaa:properties.description: How completion of this action will be verified

relationships.name: Has Gemba Observation
relationships.description: A Kaizen event includes Gemba observations from the walk. At least one observation is required before Analyze activities begin (BR-K-002).

relationships.name: Identifies Waste
relationships.description: A Gemba observation identifies exactly one waste type from the DOWNTIME taxonomy

relationships.name: Targets Waste
relationships.description: A Kaizen event targets one or more primary waste types for elimination

relationships.name: Maps Current State
relationships.description: A Kaizen event produces a current-state value stream map

relationships.name: Maps Future State
relationships.description: A Kaizen event produces a future-state value stream map

relationships.name: Runs Experiment
relationships.description: A Kaizen event runs rapid try-storm experiments during the Improve phase

relationships.name: Implements Quick Win
relationships.description: A successful rapid experiment leads to one or more quick wins implemented same-day

relationships.name: Eliminates Waste
relationships.description: A quick win eliminates or reduces a specific waste category

relationships.name: Controlled By
relationships.description: Every quick win must be sustained by a visual control mechanism (BR-K-003)

relationships.name: Has Follow Up
relationships.description: A Kaizen event generates follow-up actions for the 30-day review period

relationships.name: Follow Up For
relationships.description: A follow-up action is linked to a specific quick win for ongoing monitoring

relationships.name: VSM Reveals Waste
relationships.description: Value stream map analysis identifies waste categories in the process flow

relationships.name: Improves Measure
relationships.description: A Kaizen event improves one or more PE-ONT process metrics. Cross-ontology bridge to PE-ONT.

relationships.name: Augmented By Agent
relationships.description: A Kaizen event is augmented by AI agents for analysis, waste detection, or experiment optimisation. Cross-ontology bridge to PE-ONT.

relationships.name: Invokes Skill
relationships.description: A Kaizen event invokes skills during execution (e.g. statistical analysis, waste classification). Cross-ontology bridge to PE-ONT.

relationships.name: Applies To Process
relationships.description: A Kaizen event applies to (improves) a specific PE-ONT process. Cross-ontology bridge to PE-ONT.

relationships.name: Event Implements Pattern
relationships.description: A Kaizen event implements reusable process patterns (e.g. DMAIC cycle, PDCA subcycle). Cross-ontology bridge to PE-ONT.

relationships.name: Experiment Tests Hypothesis
relationships.description: A rapid experiment tests a formal PE-ONT hypothesis. Cross-ontology bridge to PE-ONT.

businessRules.name: Event Duration Constraint
businessRules.description: Kaizen events must be between 1 and 5 days. Events longer than 5 days should use standard DMAIC.
businessRules.action: THEN KaizenEvent.duration MUST be between P1D and P5D inclusive

businessRules.name: Gemba Before Analyze
businessRules.description: At least one Gemba observation must be recorded before Analyze activities can begin
businessRules.condition: IF a KaizenEvent transitions to Analyze phase activities
businessRules.action: THEN the event MUST have at least one hasGembaObservation relationship

businessRules.name: QuickWin Requires Visual Control
businessRules.description: Every quick win must be sustained by exactly one visual control to prevent regression
businessRules.action: THEN the QuickWin MUST have exactly one controlledBy relationship to a VisualControl

businessRules.name: Follow-Up Deadline Constraint
businessRules.description: Follow-up action deadlines must be within 30 days of event end date
businessRules.condition: IF a FollowUpAction has a deadline
businessRules.action: THEN FollowUpAction.deadline MUST be within 30 calendar days of the parent KaizenEvent.endDate

businessRules.name: Both VSMs Required Before Improve
businessRules.description: Both current-state and future-state value stream maps must exist before Improve phase
businessRules.condition: IF a KaizenEvent transitions to Improve phase activities
businessRules.action: THEN the event MUST have both mapsCurrentState (mapType=current-state) AND mapsFutureState (mapType=future-state) relationships

businessRules.name: Team Size Advisory
businessRules.description: Kaizen teams should be 5-10 members for effectiveness. Smaller teams lack diversity; larger teams reduce focus.
businessRules.condition: IF a KaizenEvent has teamSize < 5 OR teamSize > 10
businessRules.action: THEN system SHOULD generate advisory warning about suboptimal team size

businessRules.name: Overdue Follow-Up Escalation
businessRules.description: Overdue follow-up actions must trigger escalation to event sponsor
businessRules.action: THEN system MUST trigger escalation notification to event sponsor

businessRules.name: Experiment Duration Advisory
businessRules.description: Rapid experiments should not exceed 1 day. Longer experiments belong in standard DMAIC.
businessRules.condition: IF a RapidExperiment has duration > P1D
businessRules.action: THEN system SHOULD generate advisory warning recommending standard DMAIC for longer experiments

competencyQuestions.question: What wastes were observed during the Gemba walk and how severe are they?
What is the value-added ratio from the current-state VSM and where is the bottleneck?
Which rapid experiments succeeded and became quick wins?
Does every quick win have a visual control ensuring sustainability?
What follow-up actions are outstanding from the 30-day review?
What was the before/after sigma level improvement from the Kaizen event?
Which PE-ONT process metrics improved as a result of this Kaizen event?
Which waste categories are most frequently observed across multiple Kaizen events?

testInstances.description: Work that contains errors, rework, scrap, or corrections. Any output that does not meet the standard the first time.
testInstances.examples: Rework, scrap, warranty claims, data entry errors, code bugs, incorrect orders
testInstances.detectionMethod: Quality inspection data, defect logs, rework tracking, customer complaints, error rate monitoring

testInstances.description: Producing more than needed, earlier than needed, or faster than needed. The most harmful waste as it generates all other wastes.
testInstances.examples: Batch processing beyond demand, printing reports nobody reads, building features before validation, excess inventory
testInstances.detectionMethod: Compare production rate to actual demand, check for accumulating WIP, monitor batch sizes vs pull signals

testInstances.description: Idle time when people, materials, information, or equipment are not being used productively. Includes queue time and delays.
testInstances.examples: Waiting for approvals, system downtime, queue time between steps, waiting for materials, batch-and-queue delays
testInstances.detectionMethod: Time studies, queue length monitoring, idle time tracking, lead time vs process time ratio

testInstances.description: Underusing people's skills, knowledge, creativity, and experience. Knowledge silos and lack of employee engagement.
testInstances.examples: Not involving frontline workers in improvement, knowledge silos, underqualified assignments, unused suggestions
testInstances.detectionMethod: Skills gap analysis, employee engagement surveys, suggestion system utilisation, cross-training coverage

testInstances.description: Unnecessary movement of materials, products, information, or data between process steps. Does not add value.
testInstances.examples: Moving materials between buildings, email forwarding chains, multiple handoffs, data re-entry across systems
testInstances.detectionMethod: Spaghetti diagrams, material flow analysis, handoff counting, distance tracking

testInstances.description: Excess raw materials, work-in-progress, finished goods, or information beyond what is immediately needed.
testInstances.examples: Excess stock, large WIP buffers, unread email backlogs, open ticket backlogs, undeployed code branches
testInstances.detectionMethod: Inventory counts, WIP limits monitoring, backlog size tracking, storage space utilisation

testInstances.description: Unnecessary movement of people — walking, reaching, bending, searching — that does not add value to the product or service.
testInstances.examples: Walking to distant printers, searching for tools/files, switching between applications, excessive clicking
testInstances.detectionMethod: Motion studies, workplace layout analysis, click-path tracking, ergonomic assessment

testInstances.description: Doing more work than required to meet customer needs. Over-engineering, redundant steps, unnecessary approvals, gold-plating.
testInstances.examples: Unnecessary approvals, over-specification, redundant data entry, excessive documentation, gold-plating features
testInstances.detectionMethod: Value-add analysis on each process step, customer requirements comparison, approval chain review

changeControl.changeNotes: v2.0.0: PE-L6S sub-series extraction. Deprecated kdmaic:WasteCategory (replaced by vana:WasteCategory from VANA-ONT v1.0.0) and kdmaic:ValueStreamMap (replaced by vsm:ValueStreamMap from VSM-ONT v1.0.0). Added imports for VSM-ONT v1.0.0 and VANA-ONT v1.0.0. Backward-compatible: deprecated entity aliases resolve to imported equivalents. 8 predefined DOWNTIME instances preserved as test data. All other entities unchanged.

versionHistory.notes: Initial creation. 8 entity types, 18 relationships, 8 business rules, 3 join patterns, 8 DOWNTIME instances.
